title: Diagnose a Connection Pool Exhaustion Leak
description: A session-service leaks database connections because a new code path doesn't close connections in the error branch.
alert:
session-service OOMKilled + connection pool exhaustion
Container memory grew from 180MB to 1.9GB. DB connection count: 4800 (limit: 5000).
initial_log_excerpt:
[session-service] INFO service boot complete
[session-service] INFO session refresh started for batch
[session-service] WARN active DB connections: 4800/5000

session-service: [session-service] INFO session refresh started for batch
[session-service] ERROR failed to acquire connection: pool exhausted
[session-service] WARN session refresh error branch hit — connection not returned to pool

deployment_history.summary: session-service: add session refresh with retry on failure

diffs.deploy-312:
diff --git a/session-service/refresh.py b/session-service/refresh.py
+def refresh_session(session_id):
+    conn = pool.acquire()
+    try:
+        result = conn.execute('UPDATE sessions SET ...')
+        if result.error:
+            log.warn('refresh failed, retrying')
+            return refresh_session(session_id)  # recursive retry without releasing conn
+        conn.release()
+    except Exception:
+        raise  # conn never released on exception path

2026-04-07T10:40:00Z.note: PooledConnection instances are not being returned — recursive retry acquires new connection without releasing previous one

root_cause.value: connection not released on error or recursive retry

accepted_remediations: replace recursion with loop and single connection
